description.en: UnionPay is a Chinese payment card network and financial services company. UnionPay International supports global acceptance of UnionPay cards for in-store and online payments.

content_features.title: Global card acceptance
content_features.description: UnionPay cards are accepted across many countries and regions via local acquiring partners.

content_features.title: In-store and online payments
content_features.description: Works for both point-of-sale transactions and e-commerce checkout when enabled by your PSP/acquirer.

content_features.title: Card types supported
content_features.description: Support for debit, credit, and prepaid UnionPay-branded cards (availability depends on issuer and market).

content_features.title: Authorization quality and declines
content_features.description: Use accurate transaction data (amount, MCC, location, cardholder info) to reduce unnecessary declines.

content_features.title: Disputes and chargebacks flow
content_features.description: UnionPay transactions follow standard card-network processes for disputes and chargebacks through your acquirer/PSP.

content_features.title: Authentication and security
content_features.description: Add network-appropriate authentication where available via your PSP/acquirer to improve risk outcomes.

content_features.title: Cross-border optimization
content_features.description: Optimize routing and configuration with your PSP/acquirer for cross-border use cases and regional performance.

tip: For better approval rates, send complete billing and cardholder data and use the correct MCC/merchant location settings with your PSP/acquirer.

faq.question: Is UnionPay a PSP or an acquirer?
faq.answer: UnionPay is primarily a card network. You typically access it through a PSP, gateway, or acquiring bank that supports UnionPay.

faq.question: How do I start accepting UnionPay cards?
faq.answer: Enable UnionPay through your payment processor/PSP or acquiring bank, then confirm it is active for your markets and channels (in-store/online).

faq.question: Why are some UnionPay transactions declined?
faq.answer: Common causes include missing/invalid transaction data, issuer restrictions, authentication requirements, cross-border controls, or configuration/routing issues at your PSP/acquirer.

faq.question: Does UnionPay support online payments?
faq.answer: Yes, UnionPay can be used for e-commerce when supported by your PSP/acquirer and enabled for your merchant account.

faq.question: How are disputes handled for UnionPay?
faq.answer: Disputes are managed through the card network chargeback process. Your acquirer/PSP receives and responds to chargebacks on your behalf.

faq.question: Where is UnionPay available?
faq.answer: UnionPay is a global card network with broad international acceptance; availability for a specific merchant depends on your PSP/acquirer and target markets.

seo.seo_title: UnionPay payments: acceptance, setup, and declines
seo.seo_description: UnionPay is a global card network. Learn how to enable UnionPay acceptance via your PSP/acquirer for online and in-store payments.
seo.keywords: UnionPay, card network, China payments, card acceptance, ecommerce payments, POS payments, chargebacks
